calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-20-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel B. Newbold for petitioner Blake Martini (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Letters of Administration.

Mr. Newbold provided an update regarding the sale of real property in Piedmont. He stated the property remains under contract and is subject to zoning entitlement approvals by the city. He explained that entitlement work had been completed and submitted to the city and that the application was currently under review.

The Court noted that the Letters of Special Administration had expired in November and inquired whether new letters had been issued or an extension requested. Mr. Newbold stated that no formal request to extend the special administrator's authority had been filed. The Court indicated that appointment of a general administrator was not in the best interest of the parties given the limited purpose of the pending transaction. Counsel requested that the Court instead reappoint a special administrator with limited authority.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant to appointment of a special administrator for a period of six months, limited to the purposes set forth in Attachment 3d(2) of the original request. Mr. Newbold was directed to submit a proposed order consistent with the Court's ruling. The Court waived the local rule requiring declinations from the other heirs for purposes of the limited appointment.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Continued To Dec-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel B. Newfold for petitioner Blake Martini (via Zoom).
Cara Lankford for special administrators Mark Unger and Matthew Schmelzer (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Letters of Administration.

Mr. Newfold reported that a declaration had been submitted addressing the examiner's notes and that most of the issues had been resolved except for providing proper notice to the Public Administrator. He requested a short continuance to allow proper notice to be completed and to permit the special administrators to continue resolving matters related to the sale of the estate's real property.

The Court continued the matter to December 24, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
